differential_diagnoses: Congenital pulmonary airway malformation (CPAM) Type II or III
Pulmonary hypoplasia associated with obstructive uropathy
Necrotizing pneumonia or post-infectious lung abscess
next_steps: Contrast-enhanced CT scan of the chest (or MRI) to delineate the anatomy of the LLL opacity and identify potential systemic arterial supply
Echocardiogram specifically focused on the descending thoracic aorta to rule out systemic arterial supply to the lung (sequestration)
Consultation with pediatric surgery for possible surgical management or biopsy
Formal renal nuclear medicine scan (MAG3) to assess the function of the right kidney
Fiberoptic bronchoscopy to assess for underlying bronchial obstruction or structural anomaly
Repeat serial inflammatory markers and blood cultures to rule out persistent sequestered infection
